# Bylaws of Economics of Generative AI Research Lab

## Article 1. Name

The name of this corporation is `Economics of Generative AI Research Lab`.

## Article 2. Offices

The principal office of the corporation shall be located at such place in California as the Board of Directors may determine. The corporation may maintain such other offices as the Board may establish.

## Article 3. Purpose and limitations

This corporation is organized and shall be operated exclusively for charitable, educational, and scientific purposes within the meaning of Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code.

No part of the net earnings of this corporation shall inure to the benefit of, or be distributable to, any private person, except that the corporation may pay reasonable compensation for services rendered and make payments in furtherance of its exempt purposes.

No substantial part of the activities of this corporation shall consist of carrying on propaganda or otherwise attempting to influence legislation, except as permitted under Section 501(c)(3), and the corporation shall not participate or intervene in any political campaign on behalf of or in opposition to any candidate for public office.

The corporation shall not engage in activities not permitted to be carried on by a corporation exempt from federal income tax under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code.

## Article 4. No members

This corporation shall have no members within the meaning of the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law.

## Article 5. Board of Directors

The corporation shall be governed by a Board of Directors.

The authorized number of directors shall be not less than 3 and not more than 7, with the exact number to be fixed from time to time by Board resolution.

Directors shall serve terms of 2 years and may be re-elected.

## Article 6. Powers of the Board

Subject to law and the Articles of Incorporation, all corporate powers shall be exercised by or under the authority of the Board, and the business and affairs of the corporation shall be managed under the direction of the Board.

## Article 7. Meetings

Regular meetings of the Board may be held at such time and place as the Board determines.

Special meetings may be called by the President, the Secretary, or any 2 directors.

Notice of special meetings shall be given at least 4 days before the meeting by mail or 48 hours before the meeting by electronic transmission, personal delivery, or telephone.

Meetings may be held by conference telephone, video, or similar communications equipment so long as all directors can hear one another.

## Article 8. Quorum and voting

A majority of the authorized number of directors then in office shall constitute a quorum.

If a quorum is present, the act of a majority of directors present shall be the act of the Board, unless a greater vote is required by law, the Articles, or these Bylaws.

## Article 9. Action without meeting

Any action required or permitted to be taken by the Board may be taken without a meeting if all directors consent in writing or by electronic transmission.

## Article 10. Vacancies

Any vacancy on the Board may be filled by approval of the Board or, if the number of directors then in office is less than a quorum, by unanimous written consent of the directors then in office.

## Article 11. Officers

The officers of the corporation shall be a President, a Secretary, and a Treasurer, and may include such other officers as the Board appoints.

The same person may hold any number of offices except that neither the Secretary nor the Treasurer may serve concurrently as President.

### President

The President shall be the chief executive officer unless the Board provides otherwise and shall supervise the general affairs of the corporation.

### Secretary

The Secretary shall keep or cause to be kept the minutes of proceedings of the Board, maintain corporate records, and give required notices.

### Treasurer

The Treasurer shall oversee the corporation's financial affairs, books, reports, and funds.

## Article 12. Committees

The Board may create committees to the extent permitted by California law. No committee may exercise the authority of the Board except as expressly allowed by law and Board resolution.

## Article 13. Conflict of interest

The corporation shall maintain a conflict of interest policy applicable to directors, officers, committee members with board-delegated powers, and key employees. Interested-director transactions shall be reviewed and approved only in accordance with California law and applicable federal tax-exemption requirements.

## Article 14. Compensation

Directors shall not receive compensation for service as directors unless specifically approved by the Board in compliance with the corporation's compensation review policy and applicable law. Directors may receive reasonable reimbursement of expenses.

Any compensation paid by the corporation to officers, employees, contractors, or other insiders shall be reasonable and in furtherance of the corporation's exempt purposes.

## Article 15. Indemnification

To the fullest extent permitted by California law, the corporation shall indemnify its directors, officers, employees, and agents for acts performed in good faith in the course and scope of their service to the corporation.

## Article 16. Corporate records

The corporation shall maintain correct books and records of account, minutes of proceedings of the Board and committees, and such records as required by law.

## Article 17. Fiscal year

The fiscal year of the corporation shall end on the date fixed by Board resolution.

## Article 18. Amendment

These Bylaws may be amended by approval of the Board, subject to the Articles of Incorporation and applicable law.
